item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mp3 broke because of flash, excellent scp though!

item.title: Broken Audio
item.content:encoded: So not sure why but the files main location moved and since I am accessing this from iOS can we upgrade the audio player from flash to html5 possibly? I tried but it still wouldn’t play on iOS so I cancelled the changes.
Congratulations, Dr Reach

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That audio recording gave me the chills, god damn. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The red box insets are sort of borked on mobile, they appear shifted to the right, breaking page flow up. Not a significant issue (the audio not working on Chrome Mobile is, however), but still

Also, no vote. I understand why someone may like it, but it does nothing for me, because it's another straightforward "thing which makes you kill yourself", except with extra conditions (it makes you see tentacles and think the world will end and you gotta kill yourself next to your son, who will thus be infected by the same stuff, etc etc).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some things I found among the SCP with the number over 2000 is that, they tend to be very 'grandiose' and convey very subtle, if not hidden, messages. In my perspective this is often much confusing and leads to so-called WTF moment.
This SCP is yet another example, I'm afraid. To be specific, after I read the article I felt 'uh OK, so father is sacrificing himself to stop the end of the world.. wait a minute but didn't he try to get at his son?… oh wait that's because he has to die close to his son… uh… why was that?' 'ok this black thingy appears in a shape of sinew or vein on the object.. and there's some humanoid things.. laugh.. yeah there's always some laughter… what the [EXPLETIVES REDACTED] is happening?'
You may criticize my incapability to read between the lines, but at least that's how I felt, and this isn't the first nor it is the last. And this is definitely something that prevents me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really liked this. The emotional hook and very human angle made it work for me (I'm not a father myself, but I can see where this really hits the 'adult fear' button pretty hard!).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first +1 since joining the site! Got the idea of the anomaly across without destroying the effect by shining too much light on it; instead, focused on the human angle which really sells it. The audio is great but I'd agree with the previous comment that there's maybe a bit too much laughter towards the end for my tastes. Overall, really enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's completely fair, and I don't mean to argue that everything in an article has to be perfect.

I should probably have worded it something like: The father/son/suicide/apocalypse angle and the interview dialogue didn't do it for me, and the voice recording, while quite good, didn't get me past the text.

(Also, when I say "Unpopular opinion," I mostly mean "Congratulations," since you seem to have affected a lot of people :) )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"human serum albumin"

Nnno, albumin is a normal protein in human (and mammal, as far as I know) blood. Human serum is human blood with no clotting agents nor cells (white and red). The fact that it had zero clotting agents is irrelevant. The fact that it had no white or red cells is quite telling of what the ash actually is, really.

I think you mean "consist of".

Feexed.

And it's a thing that makes so creepy evil tentacles appear and destroy the world if you don't appease it in a horrific way.

Eh… well, it's more like cracks or veins filling cracks, then from the cracks come…

(…) humanoid protrusions (…)

Which, yes, are spoopy ash people. In any case, the anomaly itself is barely creepy. I liked this idea precisely because you have to traumatize your child (and doom him).

Thanks for your feedback, Litfried. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
human serum, extremely thickened with ash

Had to look that one up; Wikipedia says it's "human serum albumin".

SCP-2073 events consist on multiple Localized Reality Shifts

I think you mean "consist of".

Overall… not sure about this one. The dialogue is great in some places and pretty wonky in others, which put me off. And it's a thing that makes so creepy evil tentacles appear and destroy the world if you don't appease it in a horrific way. That said, it's still quite compelling, with the awfulness of having to traumatise your children, and I like heartless bastard Foundation.

So no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well here is one that I fail to really get, maybe I'm missing something. I'm also really not too fond of the grimdark "let's make lives miserable to test this thing" Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I really do like this, but I gotta admit, I cannot make heads or tails of that last collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cisgender is not a term for straight people. It is a fairly clinical term for people who are not transgender. It is not at all comparable to that racial slur. And I'm not going to lie, I started getting interested when that word was used. It showed, like, an awareness of gender and how it works with people. Specifying that it only occurs in cisgender males is actually really good and important to the work.
Everything else ruled. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now, correct me if i'm wrong, but i'm not sure that "cisgender" is a good word to use in a clinical context. I might be mistaken, but isn't there still a lot of controversy over whether or not gender dysphoria is a type of mental illness or not? Plus, I've seen it used a bunch of times in a negative context. You know, some people use it like "nigger" for straight people. I mean, it's up to you if you want to leave it in, but I think that just changing the addendum around so that it doesn't include the term would really only stand to improve the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ultimately… -1. Unpopular opinion, but the elements just don't gel for me.

Fair points. Thanks for your feedback. :)

Minor point: You never defined -B in the description, it just appears further down.

Fixed. It was a remnant of the original article, which did include a SCP-XXXX-B.

Edit: Also,

I don't think the basic action of the anomaly or the interview were strong enough to hold up the article, were the audio file not there.

An anomaly is just meat. Flavoring matters here, and the only way I saw to spice it up was to add the recording. Of course, it is a matter of taste, so I will not censor yours, but do you think that the rest of items in the main list can be considered "strong enough to hold even without that one thing that makes it great?" You may say well, yes, but in this case you're merely repeating a part of the interview log, which makes this one thing less interesting; again, it's a matter of opinion, but I believe the content added by that little element is enough to trap the reader further and make'em shake.

For the author, nothing is more valuable, I suppose, than to provoke emotion while telling a story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:
- I really liked your voice recording.
- I do enjoy grimdark implications about Foundation ethics.

Cons:
- I think the laughing actually diminished my enjoyment/the creepiness of the voice track.
- Your description of the manifestation event left me more confused than interested, even after I was able to picture it.
- I don't think the basic action of the anomaly or the interview were strong enough to hold up the article, were the audio file not there.

Minor point: You never defined -B in the description, it just appears further down.

Ultimately… -1. Unpopular opinion, but the elements just don't gel for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first SCP that has actually managed to scare me in god knows how long.
Kudos to you, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At its core, this is a thing that kills you, and if it doesn't kill you, it ends the world.

But, fuck, it's a damn good thing that kills you. An excellent acting performance being the clincher of an SCP is an excellent sign not just of your creativity and ability, but where we're going as a site and a community.

Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: Re: SCP-2073 ; One Last Lullaby.
item.content:encoded:
who could somehow stand me when I was in a rancorous mood.

Anger is always natural when it comes to writing an SCP. :P

Nah, just kidding. Not really. This was chilling and spooky, definietly fit for the Halloween spirit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I wasn't too sure about it but the more I read the more I liked it. Especially the collapsible. I kind of like the grim cold factor that the Foundation is planning on breeding the subject with a female to produce a first born son and study the anomaly some more. That's definitely something they would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very enjoyable read. Especially liked the recording and collapsible at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The recording gave me the chills, and the formatting is also very well done. Congratulations on that.

I don't have much to say about this, so i'm going to be succint: Even though you don't describe much of the humanoid at first, you really get to picture him exactly as i think you wanted him to be: A protective entity, that serves a bold life sentence and knows that it will eventually condemn someone that he cares about because it is fated to do so.

Very well done. Upvoted.

item.title: SCP-2073 ; One Last Lullaby.
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this one is certainly not what I wanted to make of it, but here we go. Let's see how well/poorly it does.

Credits
Credits


So many people on chat. Particularly Cimmerian, who could somehow stand me when I was in a rancorous mood; I'm not certain of how many of you reviewers will like it as it stands, but really, thank you all.

The sound file was built by piling together a few recorded sounds, my voice and laugh and a few free-to-use stuff.

Go ahead and bring some feedback, lads and lasses and assorted human beings. Or non-humans.
